description: select_past_note emits a hardcoded snapshot where editing.status = 'editing' and feed.visibleNoteIds = [] (via empty_feed()). REQ-FEED-021 requires the payload to carry 'the current application state at emit time' — specifically 'feed.visibleNoteIds, editing.*, and delete.* carry the current application state'. The Rust handler has no access to the actual Feed aggregate state (visibleNoteIds is always []), so every EditingStateChanged event emitted by select_past_note will reset the TypeScript client's feed.visibleNoteIds to an empty array. The feedReducer in TS will apply DomainSnapshotReceived with visibleNoteIds=[] wiping out the populated feed list. This is a state-management correctness defect: the TS client's feed list will be cleared every time a note is selected. REQ-FEED-021 acceptance criterion 'The payload MUST conform to FeedDomainSnapshot structure' is satisfied syntactically but violated semantically because of the hardcoded empty visibleNoteIds.
crossReference: specs/behavioral-spec.md REQ-FEED-021 — 'feed.visibleNoteIds carries the current application state at emit time'
